offer.what: Faith-based voice declaration app — users browse a library of scripture-rooted declarations, record themselves speaking God's Word over their own lives, and build a consistent daily practice with reminder scheduling
offer.coreOutcome: Consistent daily declaration of God's Word that transforms the believer from the inside out — replacing spiritual distance and doubt with lived-out peace, identity, and promise
offer.pricePoint: Free forever (foundational tier) · Silver $2.99/mo or $24/yr (save 33%) · Bronze & Gold tiers also available
offer.commitment: Self-serve freemium; monthly or annual paid subscriptions, cancel anytime
leadMagnets: Permanent free plan (no credit card) — foundational declarations library, voice recording, 4 default declaration lists, custom reminders

audience.icp: Christian believers (any denomination) who feel spiritually stuck or distant from God despite attending church, reading the Bible, and serving — they have head knowledge but lack a consistent, embodied speaking practice
painPoints: God feels far away despite doing everything right (church, Bible reading, serving)
Knowing about God intellectually but not experiencing His promises in daily life
Spiritual inconsistency — wanting a daily rhythm but failing to sustain one
The quiet voice of doubt, shame, or unworthiness goes unopposed
Never taught to speak the Word aloud as a spiritual discipline (hagah)
segments: Spiritually dormant Christians ready to reactivate a faith practice
Believers in active life crisis (marriage, health, provision, identity) seeking breakthrough
Consistent churchgoers who feel something is missing from their private practice
New or returning believers building foundational daily habits

salesCycle.trial: Free plan is permanent freemium — no time limit, no credit card required; paid upgrade happens when user 'feels the difference'
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Self-serve — free email or Google sign-up on web app; upgrade to paid plan inside the app
salesCycle.topObjection: 'I already read my Bible / go to church' — the brand pre-empts this by teaching that reading alone ≠ the promise; Joshua 1:8 frames speaking as the condition for prosperity and success, differentiating the practice from mere knowledge

ahaMoment: The first time a user records themselves declaring scripture and plays it back — hearing their own voice speak God's Word over their life creates an emotional and spiritual shift that silent reading cannot produce

retention.recurring: Monthly or annual paid subscription (Silver/Bronze/Gold); daily in-app reminder system builds habitual opens
retention.expansion: Free → Silver → Bronze → Gold tier upgrades; annual plan upsell for 33% savings over monthly
retention.churnRisk: Users who sign up but never build the daily declaration habit — if they don't make their first recording and enable reminders within the first session, they disengage before the aha moment

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — guide new users to their first recording and first reminder within 24–48 hours of signup (the activation event)
Lead-Magnet Nurture — free-user drip teaching the Joshua 1:8 premise and building urgency for the first paid upgrade
Trial/Consult Conversion — free-to-paid conversion sequence triggered after first recording or 7 days of free usage
Value Newsletter — weekly devotional or scripture-based email keeping free and paid users engaged between app sessions
Renewal, Anniversary & Expansion — annual renewal reminders, streak/milestone celebrations, upsell to higher tiers
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — sequence for users who have not opened the app in 7–14 days
toneVoice: Personal, pastoral, and earnest — confessional founder storytelling (failed marriages, spiritual struggle), scripture-anchored reasoning, direct challenges ('Every day you don't declare, that other voice gets another unopposed rep'), warm but urgent; never preachy or denominationally exclusive
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / GDPR compliance required. No healthcare, finance, or legal vertical constraints. Faith content should remain grounded in the app's own scriptural framing (Joshua 1:8) — avoid prosperity-gospel overreach or guarantee language around outcomes.
